react.answer: ## Building a Pagination Component in React **Mental model:** Pagination is a controlled component: the parent owns `page`, and the component renders page buttons + prev/next, emitting `onChange`. The only real logic is *which page numbers to show* — for many pages you show the first, last, current ±1, and ellipses for the gaps. ### Step 1 — Controlled props ```jsx function Pagination({ page, total, onChange, siblings = 1 }) { ... } ``` ### Step 2 — Build the page range with ellipses Always include 1 and `total`; around `page` include `siblings` on each side; fill gaps with a `'…'` marker. ```jsx const range = []; for (let p = 1; p <= total; p++) { if (p === 1 || p === total || Math.abs(p - page) <= siblings) range.push(p); else if (range[range.length - 1] !== '…') range.push('…'); } ``` ### Step 3 — Render numbers vs ellipsis Numbers are clickable; `'…'` is inert text. ```jsx range.map((p, i) => p === '…' ? … : ) ``` ### Step 4 — Prev / Next with bounds Disable at the edges and clamp. ```jsx ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Few pages | No ellipsis appears — the range is contiguous. | | Current at an edge | First/last are always kept, so no duplicate. | | total = 1 | Single button, both arrows disabled. | **Interview tip:** The ellipsis range builder is the meat of this question — walk through it out loud and note that always pinning page 1 and `total` is what prevents jumpy layouts as the user pages through.

vue.answer: ## Building a Pagination Component in Vue 3 **Mental model:** Pagination is controlled via `v-model` over the current page. The component renders prev/next plus page buttons; the only real logic is *which page numbers to show* — first, last, current ±siblings, with ellipses for gaps. A `computed` derives that range. ### Step 1 — Controlled with v-model ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Derive the range with computed Always include 1 and `total`; include `siblings` around the current page; fill gaps with `'…'`. ```js const range = computed(() => { const r = []; for (let p = 1; p <= props.total; p++) { if (p === 1 || p === props.total || Math.abs(p - props.modelValue) <= props.siblings) r.push(p); else if (r[r.length - 1] !== '…') r.push('…'); } return r; }); ``` ### Step 3 — Render numbers vs ellipsis ```vue ``` ### Step 4 — Prev / Next with bounds ```vue ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Few pages | Range is contiguous, no ellipsis. | | Current at edge | First/last always kept; no duplicates. | | total = 1 | Render nothing (or a single inert button). | **Interview tip:** Keeping the range in a `computed` means it recalculates only when `page`/`total`/`siblings` change — explain that caching benefit rather than computing it inline in the template.
